title: Process groups — pg (scope-based groups, cross-node propagation)
purpose: The pg module provides named process groups that span the cluster. A process can join one or more named groups; other processes can query group membership. Group changes propagate to all connected nodes. pg replaced the older pg2 module in OTP 23+.
task: Implement the pg module with scope-based groups and cross-node membership propagation.

requirements.title: Local process groups
requirements.spec: Implement local group membership: join, leave, get_members, get_local_members.
acceptance: pg:join(Group, Pid) adds Pid to the named group
pg:leave(Group, Pid) removes Pid from the group
pg:get_members(Group) returns all members (local and remote)
A process can be in multiple groups
A process leaving or dying is automatically removed from all groups
Test: join, get_members, leave, verify membership

requirements.title: Cross-node group propagation
requirements.spec: Group membership changes propagate to all connected nodes.
acceptance: When a process joins a group, the join is broadcast to all connected nodes
Remote nodes update their group membership tables
When a process leaves or dies, the leave is broadcast
Node disconnection removes all members from the disconnected node
Test: process on A joins group, node B sees the member in get_members

requirements.title: Scoped groups
requirements.spec: pg supports scopes — isolated group namespaces. The default scope is 'pg'.
acceptance: Groups in different scopes are independent
Default scope 'pg' is available without explicit start
Test: two scopes, same group name, different members

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement pg2 (deprecated predecessor) — only pg (OTP 23+)
SHALL NOT implement pg monitors (monitoring group membership changes) in this brief
SHALL handle network partitions by removing unreachable members
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
